meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Finish Your First AI App
content.seo_description: Learn to overcome stalled coding projects with professional project management. A beginner's guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your app.
content.answer_block: To finish your projects, stop coding in a vacuum. Break your vision into atomic, 2-hour tasks using a structured roadmap. By integrating Antigravity for planning and Claude Code for execution, you create a feedback loop that forces progress, ensures technical depth, and prevents the 'infinite project' syndrome common in students.
content.intro:
You’ve been here: a folder on your desktop filled with half-finished README files and broken dependencies. You started with a vision of a world-changing app, but now it’s just a graveyard of abandoned dreams. The silence of an incomplete repository is the loudest sound in a student developer's career.

But the gap between a 'student coder' and a 'shipped engineer' isn't talent—it's project management. When you treat your code like a professional product rather than a science experiment, the friction disappears. We are going to turn that graveyard into a portfolio of shipped, production-ready software.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Lovable Product (MLP)
steps.description: Stop aiming for a full SaaS. Define the one core feature that provides actual value. This minimizes scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Analyze my project idea and break it down into an MLP consisting of only 3 core user stories. Focus on simplicity and speed to ship.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project scope and identify the top 3 features required for an MVP.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how your data flows. A solid schema prevents refactoring hell later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema (SQL) for my project. Include primary keys, foreign keys, and indexes for optimal performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate the Prisma schema based on my project requirements and explain the relationships.'

steps.title: Task Granularization
steps.description: Break the project into tasks that take no longer than 90 minutes. This prevents getting stuck on 'unsolvable' bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert my project requirements into a Trello-style task list. Order them by technical dependency and difficulty.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a list of 5 actionable tasks for the current sprint, ordered by complexity.'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to execute specific tasks. Do not 'code the whole app' at once; build one module at a time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a boilerplate structure for the authentication module. Use clean, modular code practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the user authentication flow using NextAuth and Supabase as planned.'

steps.title: Automated Quality Gates
steps.description: Set up simple testing scripts. If it doesn't pass the test, it isn't finished.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test suite for the core business logic module I just built.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and execute unit tests for the auth service to ensure no regression.'

pro_tips: Never start coding until you have a written 'Definition of Done' for the current sprint.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; documentation debt is the #1 killer of student projects.
Limit your 'Research Phase' to 30 minutes. If you can't find a solution, pick a simpler path.
Commit your code every single time a test passes, no matter how small the change.
Build in public by pushing your progress to GitHub daily; the social pressure acts as a powerful motivator.
content.next_steps: After completing this, pick one feature from your roadmap, execute it using the CLI, and deploy your first build to Vercel or Netlify. Do not let the project sit idle.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy; now you need the environment. Stop building in isolation and start shipping with a community that demands excellence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
